perturbation.original_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing with each of the Reporting Persons (as such term is defined in the Schedule 13D referred to below) on behalf of each of them of a statement on Schedule 13D (including amendments thereto and statements on Schedule 13G) with respect to the Common Stock, par value $0.01 per share of Macy's, Inc., a corporation organized under the laws of the State of Delaware, and that this agreement may be included as an exhibit to such joint filing.
perturbation.changed_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing with each of the Filing Parties (as such term is defined in the Schedule 13D referred to below) on behalf of each of them of a statement on Schedule 13D (including amendments thereto and statements on Schedule 13G) with respect to the Common Stock, par value $0.01 per share of Macy's, Inc., a corporation organized under the laws of the State of Delaware, and that this agreement may be included as an exhibit to such joint filing.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Reporting Persons' to 'Filing Parties' without consistently updating it throughout the document creates confusion. While both might seem to describe the parties involved in the filing, 'Reporting Persons' is defined explicitly within the context of Schedule 13D, while 'Filing Parties' lacks this explicit tie, raising doubts about whether it refers to the same group of people. This lack of clarity is an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing with each of the Filing Parties (as such term is defined in the Schedule 13D referred to below) on behalf of each of them of a statement on Schedule 13D (including amendments thereto and statements on Schedule 13G) with respect to the Common Stock, par value $0.01 per share of Macy's, Inc., a corporation organized under the laws of the State of Delaware, and that this agreement may be included as an exhibit to such joint filing. The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of any amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the undersigned or contained herein and therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned hereby execute this agreement as of May 11, 2020. VESA EQUITY INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Marek Spurny By: Marek Spurny Title:Authorized Signatory /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title:Authorized Signatory EP INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Marek Spurny By: Marek Spurny Title:Authorized Signatory /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title:Authorized Signatory DANIEL KŘETÍNSKÝ /s/ Daniel Kretinsky By: Daniel Kretinsky
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Reporting Persons' to 'Filing Parties' creates an in-text contradiction. The original text explicitly links 'Reporting Persons' to the defined term within Schedule 13D. By changing this to 'Filing Parties' in the changed text and the contradicted text without providing a clear definition, it introduces ambiguity. This ambiguity directly contradicts the expectation of a clear and defined term, as it's uncertain whether 'Filing Parties' refers to the same entities as originally intended, as defined in the Schedule 13D. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition where interchangeable terms without definitions lead to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of any amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the undersigned or contained herein and therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others.
perturbation.changed_text: The signatories acknowledge that each shall be accountable for the timely filing of any modifications, and for the integrity and correctness of the data concerning the signatories or included herein and therein, but shall not be accountable for the integrity and correctness of the data concerning the remaining parties.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'undersigned' changed to 'signatories', 'amendments' changed to 'modifications', 'responsible' changed to 'accountable' and 'information' to 'data' introduce uncertainty regarding the parties' obligations. It's unclear if these terms mean precisely the same thing, which could lead to disputes over the extent of each party's responsibility for the accuracy and completeness of the filing. This contradiction is further compounded by the shift in language towards 'integrity and correctness', which may be interpreted differently than 'completeness and accuracy', creating a potential loophole regarding what standards apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing with each of the Reporting Persons (as such term is defined in the Schedule 13D referred to below) on behalf of each of them of a statement on Schedule 13D (including amendments thereto and statements on Schedule 13G) with respect to the Common Stock, par value $0.01 per share of Macy's, Inc., a corporation organized under the laws of the State of Delaware, and that this agreement may be included as an exhibit to such joint filing. The signatories acknowledge that each shall be accountable for the timely filing of any modifications, and for the integrity and correctness of the data concerning the signatories or included herein and therein, but shall not be accountable for the integrity and correctness of the data concerning the remaining parties. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned hereby execute this agreement as of May 11, 2020. VESA EQUITY INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Marek Spurny By: Marek Spurny Title:Authorized Signatory /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title:Authorized Signatory EP INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Marek Spurny By: Marek Spurny Title:Authorized Signatory /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title:Authorized Signatory DANIEL KŘETÍNSKÝ /s/ Daniel Kretinsky By: Daniel Kretinsky
perturbation.justification: The change from 'responsible' to 'accountable', 'information' to 'data', 'amendments' to 'modifications', and 'completeness and accuracy' to 'integrity and correctness' creates ambiguity regarding the parties' obligations, directly contradicting the expectation of clear and consistent terminology used for legal obligations within the same clause in the contradicted text. This is an example of Misaligned Terminology leading to in-text contradiction. The changed text is also placed in the same location as the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: VESA EQUITY INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Marek Spurny By: Marek Spurny Title:Authorized Signatory /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title:Authorized Signatory EP INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Marek Spurny By: Marek Spurny Title:Authorized Signatory /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title:Authorized Signatory DANIEL KŘETÍNSKÝ /s/ Daniel Kretinsky By: Daniel Kretinsky
perturbation.changed_text: VESA EQUITY INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Marek Spurny By: Marek Spurny Title: Authorized Representative /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title: Authorized Representative EP INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Marek Spurny By: Marek Spurny Title: Authorized Representative /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title: Authorized Representative DANIEL KŘETÍNSKÝ /s/ Daniel Kretinsky By: Daniel Kretinsky
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Authorized Signatory' to 'Authorized Representative' creates ambiguity. Are these roles intended to be identical in scope and authority? This is especially relevant because the agreement concerns legally binding filings. If an 'Authorized Representative' lacks the specific signatory power required for SEC filings (which an 'Authorized Signatory' presumably possesses), the agreement's validity could be challenged. This creates an in-text contradiction because it is unclear if those who are signing are truly authorized to do so, in the capacity that would make the document valid and binding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In accordance with Rule 13d-1(k)(1) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing with each of the Reporting Persons (as such term is defined in the Schedule 13D referred to below) on behalf of each of them of a statement on Schedule 13D (including amendments thereto and statements on Schedule 13G) with respect to the Common Stock, par value $0.01 per share of Macy's, Inc., a corporation organized under the laws of the State of Delaware, and that this agreement may be included as an exhibit to such joint filing. The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of any amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the undersigned or contained herein and therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned hereby execute this agreement as of May 11, 2020. VESA EQUITY INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Marek Spurny By: Marek Spurny Title: Authorized Representative /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title: Authorized Representative EP INVESTMENT S.À R.L. /s/ Marek Spurny By: Marek Spurny Title: Authorized Representative /s/ Pascal Leclerc By: Pascal Leclerc Title: Authorized Representative DANIEL KŘETÍNSKÝ /s/ Daniel Kretinsky By: Daniel Kretinsky
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Authorized Signatory' to 'Authorized Representative' introduces ambiguity regarding the individuals' authority to execute legally binding filings, specifically SEC filings. The contradicted text emphasizes compliance with Rule 13d-1(k)(1) under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, which necessitates proper authorization for joint filings. If an 'Authorized Representative' lacks the signatory power required for SEC filings, the agreement's validity could be challenged, creating a direct conflict. This contradicts the requirement for proper legal execution as mandated in the contradicted text.
